strategy_summary: Tardigrades survive near-complete drying and wide temperature extremes by protecting their cellular machinery with specialized proteins, entering a suspended state and recovering intact when conditions improve.
mechanism: Intrinsically disordered cytoplasmic-abundant heat-soluble (CAHS) proteins vitrify as water is lost, forming a glass-like matrix that immobilizes and shields proteins and membranes through hydrogen-bond replacement. This molecular gel prevents denaturation and aggregation during desiccation and thermal stress.
